body:
## Summary
- Add a non-fatal `assign-agent` CLI that derives the assignee login from `AGENT_HANDLE`, checks assignability, and assigns through the Issues API.
- Wire assignment into accepted issue/PR route workflows while keeping labels and mentions as the automation signal layer.
- Add onboarding setup visibility, docs, and focused tests.

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/assign-agent-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/onboarding-check-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/github.test.js`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #135

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR closes #135, which is explicitly stage 2 under parent #126 after #25/#134; implementation matches the parent plan to default assignment from `AGENT_HANDLE` while keeping labels as the signal layer. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | New assignment behavior is isolated in [`.agent/src/agent-assignee.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/6bd10511f48993f6219b61733fca60301ab6b8d5/.agent/src/agent-assignee.ts) and reuses existing [`.agent/src/github.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/6bd10511f48993f6219b61733fca60301ab6b8d5/.agent/src/github.ts) CLI/API helper patterns rather than embedding GitHub logic directly in workflows. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The CLI uses separate env inputs `AGENT_HANDLE`, `TARGET_KIND`, `TARGET_NUMBER`, and `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, with explicit validation for supported target kinds and valid positive numbers in [`.agent/src/cli/assign-agent.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/6bd10511f48993f6219b61733fca60301ab6b8d5/.agent/src/cli/assign-agent.ts). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | pass | 7/7 | Assignment is best-effort and uses GitHub’s assignee endpoint after assignability checks; onboarding keeps the existing setup issue/comment marker flow and only adds assignability status to that reused artifact. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Invalid handles, unassignable logins, missing repository context, and assignment API failures are surfaced as warnings while preserving non-fatal workflow behavior; tests cover invalid namespaced handles and unassignable users in [`.agent/src/__tests__/assign-agent-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/6bd10511f48993f6219b61733fca60301ab6b8d5/.agent/src/__tests__/assign-agent-cli.test.ts). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Docs were updated in [supported workflows](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/6bd10511f48993f6219b61733fca60301ab6b8d5/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md) and [configuration list](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/6bd10511f48993f6219b61733fca60301ab6b8d5/.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md) to explain `AGENT_HANDLE`-derived best-effort assignment and that labels/mentions remain the automation signal layer. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is narrowly scoped to assignment support, workflow wiring, onboarding visibility, docs, and focused tests; no unrelated refactors or broad formatting churn are present. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Documentation adds behavioral guidance inline and does not introduce per-file inventory tables. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric alignment is that the PR follows the parent issue’s exact design: derive the assignee from `AGENT_HANDLE`, check assignability, continue on failure, and keep labels/mentions as the source of truth.
- Regression coverage is focused on the new assignment contract and workflow wiring.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific violations found.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis > Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**. > Requested by @lolipopshock. ## Summary of PR/Issue - PR #136 implements issue #135, the second child task under parent issue #126’s staged plan for Project/agent workflow improvements. - The PR adds best-effort assignment of accepted Sepo work to the login derived from `AGENT_HANDLE`, defaulting `@sepo-agent` to `sepo-agent`. - It keeps labels and mentions as the automation source of truth, adds assignability checks, wires assignment into accepted issue/PR routes, updates onboarding visibility, docs, and focused tests. - The broader goal is better visibility that Sepo is handling accepted work without making GitHub assignees a hard dependency. ## Review The core assignment module, CLI behavior, GitHub API helpers, docs, and tests are broadly solid, and the rubrics review passed. One reviewer found a concrete blocker in the label-triggered router path: custom `AGENT_HANDLE` values are not propagated from `agent-label.yml`, so some accepted label-triggered work can be assigned to the wrong default handler. | Issue | Severity | Description | | -- | -- | -- | | Label-triggered router assignment ignores custom `AGENT_HANDLE` | BLOCKING | `.github/workflows/agent-router.yml` assigns from `inputs.agent_handle`, but `.github/workflows/agent-label.yml` does not pass that input. Repos with a custom handler can assign label-triggered `answer`/`skill` work to default `@sepo-agent`. | | Review workflow assignment anchor is best-effort fragile | INFO | One reviewer noted `agent-review.yml` assigns only in the `codex` matrix lane. This is acceptable for best-effort assignment, though the single-threaded synthesis job would be a more robust future anchor. | | Core implementation, docs, and rubrics are aligned | INFO | Reviewers agreed the assignment CLI/API design, non-fatal behavior, onboarding update, docs, and focused tests match issue #135’s intent. | ## Progress - Parent issue #126 and child issue #135 were reviewed; this PR matches the staged plan after activity-label work in PR #134. - Rubrics review is already posted on the PR and reports `PASS`. - Existing inline review comments were checked before posting; none existed. - Posted one inline comment on `.github/workflows/agent-router.yml` line 267 for the custom-handle label-trigger assignment gap. - No reviewer raised a blocking issue in the core assignment logic itself. ## Issue Details
Label-triggered router assignment ignores custom AGENT_HANDLE **Cause:** The new router assignment step uses `AGENT_HANDLE: ${{ inputs.agent_handle || '@sepo-agent' }}`, but label-triggered runs enter through `agent-label.yml`, which calls `agent-router.yml` without passing `agent_handle`. **Candidate solutions:** Pass `agent_handle` from `agent-label.yml` using `vars.AGENT_HANDLE || '@sepo-agent'`, or make the router step fall back to `vars.AGENT_HANDLE` when the workflow-call input is empty. Add focused coverage for a label-triggered run with a custom handle. **Comments:** Codex review treats this as blocking. Claude and rubrics review found the rest of the implementation shippable/pass, so the disagreement is scoped to this router caller gap rather than the assignment design overall.
## Recommended Next Step FIX_PR: The unresolved blocker is a small, concrete workflow wiring and test coverage change that is safe for an automated fix-pr pass. ## Final Verdict NEEDS_REWORK ## Action Items - [ ] Preserve custom `AGENT_HANDLE` for label-triggered router assignment by passing it from `agent-label.yml` or falling back to `vars.AGENT_HANDLE` in `agent-router.yml`, with focused regression coverage.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is scoped to assignment support, workflow wiring, onboarding visibility, docs, and focused tests; the follow-up fix only adds `agent_handle` propagation in `agent-label.yml` plus regression coverage. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Documentation changes are concise behavioral notes and do not add per-file inventory tables. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The design derives the assignee from existing `AGENT_HANDLE` instead of introducing `AGENT_ASSIGNEE`, and uses one small CLI plus workflow calls for the accepted routes. |

## Notes

- The earlier custom-handle label-trigger gap appears addressed in the current PR diff: `agent-label.yml` now passes `agent_handle: ${{ vars.AGENT_HANDLE || '@sepo-agent' }}` into `agent-router.yml`, and `envelope.test.ts` asserts that path.
- The implementation stays aligned with #135’s core constraints: assignment is not authoritative, failures are warnings, and existing label/mention behavior remains intact.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific violations found.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #136 implements issue #135, a child task under parent issue #126’s staged plan for agent/project workflow improvements.
- It adds best-effort assignment of accepted Sepo issue/PR work to the login derived from `AGENT_HANDLE`, defaulting `@sepo-agent` to `sepo-agent`.
- The design keeps labels and mentions as the automation source of truth, while using GitHub assignees only as a visibility convenience.
- It also updates onboarding visibility, workflow wiring, docs, and focused tests.

## Review
The overall implementation is well scoped and aligned with the parent plan, and the previous custom-handle router gap is fixed. One unresolved assignment-gating issue remains: the current assignability probe can skip assignment even when the configured handler is repo-assignable.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Assignability check can falsely skip assignment | BLOCKING | The issue-specific assignability check returns 404 for the current default handler on live issue/PR targets, while the repo-level check returns 204. Because `ghApiOk` treats that 404 as unassignable, accepted work remains unassigned. |
| Custom-handle label-trigger gap is fixed | INFO | The prior blocker is addressed by passing `agent_handle` from `agent-label.yml` into the reusable router, with envelope coverage. |

## Progress
- Broader context from #126 and #135 confirms the requested behavior: derive assignment from `AGENT_HANDLE`, keep assignment best-effort, and preserve labels/mentions as the signal layer.
- Rubrics review passed, and the PR check is passing.
- `git diff --check` passes.
- Existing inline comments were checked before posting; one new inline comment was posted on `.agent/src/github.ts` line 149 for the assignability false-negative.
- Reviewer disagreement noted: GitHub docs do document the issue-specific endpoint, so the problem is the observed false-negative for this repo’s intended assignment flow, not that the route is nonexistent. Reference: [GitHub REST issue assignees docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/issues/assignees).

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Assignability check can falsely skip assignment</summary>

**Cause:** `isIssueAssigneeAssignable` gates assignment on `repos/{repo}/issues/{number}/assignees/{assignee}`. In live checks, `repos/self-evolving/repo/assignees/sepo-agent` returns 204, but the issue-specific checks for issue #135 and PR #136 return 404, so `resolveAgentAssignee` skips before the idempotent add-assignee call.

**Candidate solutions:** Use the repo-level `repos/{owner}/{repo}/assignees/{assignee}` endpoint for this best-effort gate, or fall back to it when the issue-specific check returns 404. Update the `github.ts` helper signature, the `agent-assignee.ts` call site, and the CLI/onboarding test stubs that currently expect the issue-specific check.

**Comments:** Claude flagged this as blocking; Codex and rubrics review considered current head shippable. The synthesis validates a narrower blocker: not an undocumented endpoint, but a false-negative gate that prevents the feature from assigning accepted work in the target repo.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The unresolved blocker is a small, concrete helper/call-site/test update that is safe for automated fix-pr work.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Replace or harden the assignability gate so default `AGENT_HANDLE` assignment is not skipped by issue-specific 404s, and update focused test coverage for the chosen endpoint behavior.

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |

| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | GitHub assignment behavior is centralized in `.agent/src/agent-assignee.ts` and `.agent/src/github.ts`; workflows invoke the typed CLI instead of embedding API logic inline. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | `.agent/src/cli/assign-agent.ts` uses distinct env inputs: `AGENT_HANDLE`, `TARGET_KIND`, `TARGET_NUMBER`, and `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, with explicit validation for target kind and positive target number. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | pass | 7/7 | Assignment uses GitHub’s assignee API and repo-level assignability checks; onboarding updates the existing setup issue/comment marker rather than creating repeated artifacts. |
| communication / generic | Keep status comments compact | pass | 6/6 | Onboarding adds a single checklist line for agent-handle assignability, keeping the visible setup status compact and user-facing. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Invalid handles, missing repo context, unassignable users, and assignment API failures surface warnings while the non-fatal CLI exits successfully; focused tests cover skip/warning paths. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md` and `.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md` document best-effort assignment from `AGENT_HANDLE` and clarify labels/mentions remain the signal layer. |

## Notes

- The current diff addresses the earlier review blockers: `agent-label.yml` now passes `agent_handle` into `agent-router.yml`, and assignability now uses the repo-level assignee endpoint to avoid issue-specific false negatives.
- The implementation matches #135’s requested contract: assignment is best-effort visibility only, failures are warnings, and labels/mentions remain the automation source of truth.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific violations found.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis > Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**. > Requested by @lolipopshock. ## Summary of PR/Issue - PR #136 implements issue #135, the assign-to-agent stage under parent issue #126’s GitHub Project and agent workflow plan. - It adds best-effort assignment of accepted Sepo issue/PR work to the login derived from `AGENT_HANDLE`, defaulting `@sepo-agent` to `sepo-agent`. - The PR keeps labels and mentions as the automation source of truth, uses repo-level assignability checks, and surfaces assignment failures as non-fatal warnings. - It also updates workflow wiring, onboarding visibility, docs, and focused tests, stacked after the activity-label work from PR #134. ## Review The latest reviews agree the previous blocking issues are resolved, and the rubrics review passes. Two remaining warnings are concrete and branch-changeable: one behavioral guard in the router assignment step, and one misleading interface left after the repo-level assignability refactor. | Issue | Severity | Description | | -- | -- | -- | | Pending-approval routes can assign before acceptance | WARNING | [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/1f0e6589501a47519b98188409aba1844d984503/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml#L259-L264) assigns whenever a routed issue/PR is non-unsupported, even when triaged `implement` / `create-action` only posts an approval request. This can make Sepo look like it accepted ownership before human approval. | | `resolveAgentAssignee` keeps unused target fields | WARNING | [`resolveAgentAssignee`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/1f0e6589501a47519b98188409aba1844d984503/.agent/src/agent-assignee.ts#L46-L51) still requires `targetNumber` and `targetKind`, but the repo-level assignability check no longer uses them. The signature now misleads callers and forces onboarding to pass dummy target data. | | Previous blockers are fixed and rubrics pass | INFO | The custom `AGENT_HANDLE` label-trigger gap and issue-specific assignability false-negative were fixed. Rubrics review reports `PASS` with no rubric-specific violations. | ## Progress - Parent issue #126 and child issue #135 were reviewed; the PR matches the staged assign-agent-handle plan. - Earlier blockers were addressed: `agent-label.yml` now passes `agent_handle`, and assignability now uses the repo-level endpoint. - Reviewers reported passing checks: build, focused tests, `git diff --check`, and GitHub checks. - Existing inline comments were checked; two new inline comments were posted for the remaining warnings. ## Issue Details
Pending-approval routes can assign before acceptance **Cause:** The router assignment condition checks `should_respond`, route presence, and target kind, but not `steps.dispatch.outputs.needs_approval`. Triaged `implement` / `create-action` decisions can therefore assign the target even though the router only posts an approval request. **Candidate solutions:** Add `steps.dispatch.outputs.needs_approval != 'true'` to the assignment step condition and update `envelope.test.ts` to cover an approval-gated route. **Comments:** Codex raised this as a warning. It is not contradicted by the other reviews; rubrics still pass overall.
resolveAgentAssignee keeps unused target fields **Cause:** After switching from issue-specific to repo-level assignability, `resolveAgentAssignee` only needs `agentHandle` and `repo`, but its input type still includes `targetNumber` and `targetKind`. **Candidate solutions:** Narrow `resolveAgentAssignee` to `{ agentHandle?: string; repo: string }`, keep target fields only on `assignAgentHandleToTarget`, and update the onboarding call site/tests accordingly. **Comments:** Claude raised this as a code-quality warning. It is small, but cleaning it up prevents future misunderstanding of the assignment contract.
## Recommended Next Step FIX_PR: The remaining issues are concrete, low-risk workflow/module updates with focused test coverage paths. ## Final Verdict MINOR_ISSUES ## Action Items - [ ] Add a pending-approval guard to the router assignment step and cover that approval-gated path in tests. - [ ] Narrow `resolveAgentAssignee` to the fields it actually uses and update onboarding/tests.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.
